pullRequest.title: [DOM-54528] Create and Update APIs for Compute Providers Admin API

nodes.body:
I'm not sure if exceptions are the right thing to be throwing for validation errors, but I'm also not sure if there's a better way to aggregate errors for all fields.
Is this the right approach for scala or should I be doing some kind of Either chaining?

I think it would be ideal to 
1. return all errors for all fields
2. not use exceptions but rather normal control logic to accumulate validation failures
However I think we could capture this in a nice to have ticket for later and this is fine as a starting point

This is placeholder for now.
Do we have a pattern on how to return batched validation errors?
I'm considering creating a dto with a map/list of errors but not sure what the ideal approach is.
I can see pros & cons of both approaches.  Wrapping them into one exception seems fine to me.
This method also fetches values from the schema's default if provided. Is that something that should be possible for secrets or should we only use the configured value and ignore the default if any?
should there be a check on the length of a secret (like does vault have a max length)?

I don't see anything in our internal docs, but found this in what is hopefully the right docs page: https://developer.hashicorp.com/vault/docs/internals/limits#entity-and-group-limits

Should the length field for text entries be used for secrets as well or no?
oh, that does make sense to me -- just use the length field to validate the length of the secret like we do for a text field
